item.title: Foreclosures surged 21% in the first half of 2026, new data shows
item.description: The state with the biggest jump in foreclosure activity was Idaho, where filings increased 59% compared to the same time last year.

item.title: Nearly 736,000 Pillsbury rolls recalled over possible glass contamination
item.description: The recall includes cases of Pillsbury "Hard Roll Dough" and "Kaiser Roll Dough" bread rolls, which are marketed to businesses.

item.title: Mom of murdered woman gives emotional testimony at Todd Blanche's confirmation hearing
item.description: Antioch, Illinois, mother Jennifer Bos voiced her support for Todd Blanche at his confirmation hearing to become attorney general. Bos advocates for stricter immigration policy after her daughter was allegedly murdered by an illegal immigrant.

item.title: Cape Verde goalie talks about team's inspiring World Cup run
item.description: Cape Verde's goalie Josimar José Évora Dias, known as Vozinha, talks with "CBS Mornings" about the team's inspiring run in the World Cup. The tiny country was playing in the tournament for the first time. Vozinha describes his unexpected rise to fame, challenges to becoming a professional player and his future in soccer.

item.title: Epstein survivor describes safety concerns, job loss in emotional testimony at Blanche hearing
item.description: Dani Bensky, a survivor of Jeffrey Epstein, gave an emotional testimony before the Senate Judiciary Committee during Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche's confirmation hearing. She detailed how the release of the Epstein files resulted in privacy and safety concerns, job loss and reputational harm.

item.title: Key GOP senators undecided on Todd Blanche's AG confirmation as hearing enters Day 2
item.description: Todd Blanche's confirmation hearing continues Thursday with witness testimony, as some key Republican senators remain undecided on whether to appoint him to the permanent role as attorney general. CBS News' Jake Rosen has more.

item.title: Texas man fatally shot by ICE "was screaming for someone to help him," son says
item.description: Ronaldo Salgado and Lorenzo Salgado Jr., the sons of the Mexican man who was shot and killed by an ICE agent in Houston, spoke to CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez about their father's death.

item.title: Maps show wildfire smoke forecast, air quality alerts in swath of U.S.
item.description: Heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is engulfing large swaths of the Midwest and Northeast U.S. this week.

item.title: Trump's DNI pick Jay Clayton refuses to answer on who won the 2020 election
item.description: President Trump's pick for director of national intelligence, Jay Clayton, refused to answer who won the 2020 presidential election during a confirmation hearing. Meanwhile, Mr. Trump is expected to make a primetime speech Thursday night. CBS News' Aaron Navarro has more.

item.title: What to know ahead of Trump's primetime speech
item.description: President Trump is set to give a primetime speech Thursday night, where he's expected to accuse China of meddling in U.S. elections, sources said. CBS News' Aaron Navarro has more on what to expect from Mr. Trump's speech, and CBS News election law contributor David Becker also joins to discuss.

item.title: China bans AI chatbot relationships to combat declining birth rate
item.description: China is pushing to regulate relationships with artificial intelligence chatbots as birthrates decline. Matt Sheehan, a senior fellow at Carnegie Endowment for International Peace, explains how addictive behaviors, psychological harm and a decreasing population have motivated Chinese officials to ban AI chatbot relationships.

item.title: Zelenskyy's move to oust popular defense chief draws major backlash
item.description: Ukrainians are demonstrating in Kyiv and senior figures announcing their resignations over President Volodymyr Zelenksyy's move to oust his popular defense chief.

item.title: Argentina fans overjoyed as Messi lifts team to 2-1 World Cup victory over England
item.description: Argentina fans across the world celebrated as their team advanced to the FIFA World Cup final with a late comeback against rival England. CBS News' Nicole Valdes has more.

item.title: U.S. and Iran launch new attacks and hurl threats as war intensifies
item.description: Iran said it would attack "all infrastructure in the region" if President Trump follows through on his threats to attack Iranian civilian infrastructure.

item.title: New clues, including GPS data, emerge from Nolan Wells' death investigation
item.description: Newly obtained GPS data from the Mississippi Department of Marine Resources tracks the movements of the boat that Mississippi teen Nolan Wells was on before he went missing. Wells was found dead after a Fourth of July boat trip to Horn Island with friends. CBS News' Anna Schecter has the latest on the investigation.

item.title: DHS could weigh use of Medicaid, housing help in green card decisions
item.description: Immigration officers could weigh use of Medicaid, food aid and housing help in green card decisions after Trump administration rescinds Biden-era public charge rule.

item.title: Navy's Blue Angels investigate after video shows low flyover at Florida beach
item.description: The Blue Angels said an aircraft "flew lower than standard profiles, resulting in a disturbance on the beach that affected civilian chairs and umbrellas."

item.title: How much interest will a $5,000 no-penalty CD earn now?
item.description: Wondering if a no-penalty CD is worth it in this rate landscape? Here's what a $5,000 deposit could earn today.

item.title: "48 Hours" show schedule
item.description: True crime. Real justice. To miss it would be a crime.

item.title: From the archives: Pat Oliphant and the art of political cartoons
item.description: Pulitzer Prize-winning cartoonist Pat Oliphant, whose acidic drawings skewering political figures were syndicated in as many as 500 publications around the world, died on July 13, 2026 at age 90. In this April 16, 2000 "Sunday Morning" story, Oliphant talked with Morley Safer about caricature, censorship, and the influence of the first great political cartoonist, 19th century French master Honoré Daumier, whose grotesque drawings of King Louis Philippe led to a curtailment of press freedom in France in 1835.

item.title: Meteorite that hit home contains "alien world chemistry," experts say
item.description: When scientists examined the preserved fragments of a meteorite that crashed in 2024, they found brine-like fluids and key molecules.

item.title: Senior defense officials considering military action against Cuba
item.description: The Pentagon is drawing up plans for possible military action in Cuba, U.S. officials say. Charlie D'Agata reports.

item.title: Dozens rescued in central Texas after region hit by flash flooding for third day in a row
item.description: Torrential rain slammed central Texas for the third day in a row, triggering dangerous flash floods in parts of the region. The same storm system also spun up a tornado near San Antonio. Jason Allen has the latest.

item.title: Senior defense officials looking at Cuba military options
item.description: Military planners have examined options for possible action against Cuba, including an Army-led air assault involving the 101st Airborne Division.

item.title: George Santos to try hand as contestant in grueling TV reality show
item.description: George Santos has worn many hats: swindler, congressman​, prison inmate​, podcast host. The obvious next gig? Reality TV show contestant.

item.title: "Jurassic Park" star Sam Neill's cause of death revealed
item.description: The actor's agent said he was providing more information following news reports "which contain inaccuracies and outright falsehoods."

item.title: From Balogun's red card to an upset run, some of the World Cup's biggest stories
item.description: Spain is looking to secure a World Cup victory for the first time in 16 years, and Argentina enters Sunday hoping to become back-to-back champions.

item.title: GPS data tracks boat Nolan Wells was on before he went missing
item.description: GPS data from the Mississippi Department of Marine Resources, obtained by CBS News, tracks the movements of the boat that carried Nolan Wells to Horn Island on July 4.

item.title: California man charged in missing wife's murder left a review for spellcaster
item.description: Investigators say Larry Millete contacted spellcasters to try to put a hex on his wife Maya so she wouldn't leave him.

item.title: Sundance Film Festival moving to Boulder
item.description: The iconic Sundance Film Festival will be moving from Park City, Utah to Boulder, Colorado, starting in 2027, the festival and the Colorado Governor's Office announced on Thursday.
